items.title: Reproducing a production incident
items.scenario: An agent misbehaves in production and you need the exact dependency set locally to debug.
items.explanation: Installing from the committed lockfile rebuilds the precise versions that were running.

items.title: Stopping silent transitive drift
items.scenario: A transitive dependency releases a breaking patch overnight.
items.explanation: A lockfile pins that transitive version, so builds keep working until you deliberately re-lock.

items.title: Hash verification in CI
items.scenario: A package mirror serves a corrupted or tampered artifact.
items.explanation: Hash-pinned installs reject any download whose checksum doesn't match the lock.

items.title: Reviewing a dependency bump
items.scenario: You upgrade one library and want to see exactly what else changed.
items.explanation: Re-locking produces a diff in the lockfile that reviewers can inspect before merging.
